title: What to know about sunburn before using medicinal herbs
intro: Herbal teas, such as chamomile and calendula, offer soothing relief for sunburn. Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling, while calendula's healing properties promote skin repair. Both teas, when cooled, make an excellent gentle topical treatment for sunburn. Additionally, drinking these teas hydrates the body from within, aiding in the recovery process. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, is another excellent choice, both as a drink and a compress, to help combat sun damage and promote skin health.
definition: Sunburn is a condition where the skin becomes red, painful, and swollen after exposure to the sun's ultraviolet (UV) rays. This occurs when the skin's protective pigment, melanin, cannot produce enough defense quickly enough, resulting in damage to the skin cells. Sunburns are important to understand because they indicate that harmful UV radiation has caused DNA damage to your skin cells. Repeated DNA damage can lead to skin cancers, including deadly melanoma. Therefore, it is crucial to prevent sunburns through protective measures such as wearing sun-protective clothing, seeking shade during peak sunlight hours, and applying broad-spectrum sunscreen.
